title: Introduction to ReproStat
text: ReproStat provides tools diagnosing reproducibility statistical modeling workflows. central idea perturb data small ways (bootstrap, subsampling, noise) measure much model outputs change across perturbations. Stable outputs imply high reproducibility; volatile outputs flag potential concerns. package computes: Coefficient stability - variance estimates across perturbations. P-value stability - frequency significant findings. Selection stability - frequency variable selection behavior. Prediction stability - variance predictions. Reproducibility Index (RI) - composite 0-100 score.

title: Introduction to ReproStat
text: Three perturbation strategies supported:
code: # Bootstrap resampling (default) run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, mtcars, method = "bootstrap")  # Subsampling (80 % of rows without replacement) run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, mtcars, method = "subsample", frac = 0.8)  # Gaussian noise injection (5 % of each column's SD) run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, mtcars, method = "noise", noise_sd = 0.05)

what: Model comparison with CV ranking stability
title: Introduction to ReproStat
text: Use cv_ranking_stability() compare multiple candidate models across repeated cross-validation runs.
code: models <- list(   baseline = mpg ~ wt + hp + disp,   compact = mpg ~ wt + hp,   expanded = mpg ~ wt + hp + disp + qsec )  cv_result <- cv_ranking_stability(models, mtcars, v = 5, R = 40) cv_result$summary #>      model mean_rmse   sd_rmse mean_rank top1_frequency #> 1  compact  2.684893 0.1629443     1.050           0.95 #> 2 expanded  2.811187 0.1605096     2.425           0.05 #> 3 baseline  2.798253 0.1474168     2.525           0.00 plot_cv_stability(cv_result, metric = "top1_frequency")

title: Introduction to ReproStat
text: ReproStat supports four backends. use API; backend argument changes.
code: # Generalized linear model (logistic) run_diagnostics(   am ~ wt + hp,   mtcars,   B = 100,   family = stats::binomial() )  # Robust regression (requires MASS) if (requireNamespace("MASS", quietly = TRUE)) {   run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, mtcars, B = 100, backend = "rlm") }  # LASSO (requires glmnet) if (requireNamespace("glmnet", quietly = TRUE)) {   run_diagnostics(     mpg ~ wt + hp + disp + qsec,     mtcars,     B = 100,     backend = "glmnet",     en_alpha = 1   ) }

title: Backend Guide
text: ReproStat supports multiple model-fitting backends high-level API. means can often keep reproducibility workflow changing modeling engine. Supported backends : "lm" ordinary least squares "glm" generalized linear models "rlm" robust regression via MASS "glmnet" penalized regression via glmnet article explains use one changes returned diagnostics.

title: Backend Guide
text: entry point used across backends: key differences : model fit quantities available interpret selection-related outputs
code: run_diagnostics(   formula,   data,   B = 200,   method = "bootstrap",   backend = "lm" )

title: Backend Guide
text: "lm" default backend best place start standard linear regression. Use "lm" : response continuous ordinary least squares intended analysis want simplest interpretation components
code: diag_lm <- run_diagnostics(   mpg ~ wt + hp + disp,   data = mtcars,   B = 100,   backend = "lm" )  reproducibility_index(diag_lm) #> $index #> [1] 90.13777 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.9218519  0.9000000  0.8100000  0.9736588

title: Backend Guide
text: Use "glm" need generalized linear model, logistic Poisson regression. Notes: provide family = ... leaving backend = "lm", function promotes fit "glm" prediction stability GLMs uses response-scale predictions p-value selection summaries remain available
code: diag_glm <- run_diagnostics(   am ~ wt + hp + qsec,   data = mtcars,   B = 100,   backend = "glm",   family = stats::binomial() )  reproducibility_index(diag_glm) #> $index #> [1] 74.76995 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.2498896  1.0000000  0.8333333  0.9075752

title: Backend Guide
text: Use "rlm" want robustness outliers heavy-tailed error behavior. Use "rlm" : influential observations may distort OLS results want robust regression baseline still want coefficient, selection, prediction, RI summaries familiar regression framework
code: if (requireNamespace("MASS", quietly = TRUE)) {   diag_rlm <- run_diagnostics(     mpg ~ wt + hp + disp,     data = mtcars,     B = 100,     backend = "rlm"   )    reproducibility_index(diag_rlm) } #> Warning in rlm.default(x, y, weights, method = method, wt.method = wt.method, : #> 'rlm' failed to converge in 20 steps #> $index #> [1] 88.09575 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.9103266  0.7800000  0.8600000  0.9735035

title: Backend Guide
text: Use "glmnet" want penalized regression LASSO, ridge, elastic net. en_alpha argument controls penalty mix: 1 gives LASSO 0 gives ridge values give elastic net Important differences "glmnet": p-values defined, pvalue component NA selection stability measures non-zero selection frequency RI values therefore based different component set non-penalized backends
code: if (requireNamespace("glmnet", quietly = TRUE)) {   diag_glmnet <- run_diagnostics(     mpg ~ wt + hp + disp + qsec,     data = mtcars,     B = 100,     backend = "glmnet",     en_alpha = 1   )    reproducibility_index(diag_glmnet) } #> $index #> [1] 84.08971 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.7308482         NA  0.8250000  0.9668430

what: Comparing Models with CV Ranking Stability
title: ReproStat - Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
text: ReproStat also helps evaluate model-selection stability, just single-model stability. cv_ranking_stability() repeatedly runs cross-validation across competing formulas records often model ranks best. useful lowest average error consistently top-ranked model thing.
code: models <- list(   baseline = mpg ~ wt + hp,   medium   = mpg ~ wt + hp + disp,   full     = mpg ~ wt + hp + disp + qsec )  cv_obj <- cv_ranking_stability(models, mtcars, v = 5, R = 50) cv_obj$summary plot_cv_stability(cv_obj, metric = "top1_frequency") plot_cv_stability(cv_obj, metric = "mean_rank")

title: ReproStat - Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
code: library(ReproStat)  set.seed(42)  diag_obj <- run_diagnostics(   mpg ~ wt + hp + disp,   data = mtcars,   B = 200,   method = "bootstrap" )  # Individual summaries coef_stability(diag_obj) pvalue_stability(diag_obj) selection_stability(diag_obj) prediction_stability(diag_obj)$mean_variance  # Composite summary ri <- reproducibility_index(diag_obj) cat(sprintf("RI = %.1f\n", ri$index)) ri_confidence_interval(diag_obj, R = 500, seed = 42)  # Visuals oldpar <- par(mfrow = c(2, 2)) plot_stability(diag_obj, "coefficient") plot_stability(diag_obj, "pvalue") plot_stability(diag_obj, "selection") plot_stability(diag_obj, "prediction") par(oldpar)

code: set.seed(1) d <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, B = 50) coef_stability(d) #>  (Intercept)           wt           hp  #> 3.512876e+00 3.919577e-01 4.783342e-05

title: Cross-validation ranking stability — cv_ranking_stability
text: Evaluates model selection stability repeatedly running \(K\)-fold cross-validation recording error-metric rank candidate model across repetitions.  Supports four modeling backends: "lm", "glm", "rlm" (robust regression via MASS), "glmnet" (penalized regression via glmnet).

title: Cross-validation ranking stability — cv_ranking_stability
code: cv_ranking_stability(   formulas,   data,   v = 5L,   R = 30L,   seed = 20260307L,   family = NULL,   backend = c("lm", "glm", "rlm", "glmnet"),   en_alpha = 1,   lambda = NULL,   metric = c("auto", "rmse", "logloss") )

title: Cross-validation ranking stability — cv_ranking_stability
text: formulas named list formulas, one per candidate model. data data frame. v Number cross-validation folds. Must satisfy \(2 \le v \le n\). Default 5. R Number cross-validation repetitions. Default 30. seed Integer random seed reproducibility. Default 20260307. family GLM family object (e.g. stats::binomial()) NULL (default) use stats::lm.  Used backend = "glm". backend Modeling backend.  One "lm" (default), "glm", "rlm", "glmnet". en_alpha Elastic-net mixing parameter glmnet (default 1 LASSO). Ignored backends. lambda Regularization parameter glmnet. NULL (default), selected per fold via glmnet::cv.glmnet.  Ignored backends. metric Error metric used ranking. One "auto" (default: "rmse" lm/"rlm"/"glmnet", "logloss" glm non-Gaussian family), "rmse", "logloss".  "logloss" metric assumes binary (0/1) response; defined multi-class outcomes.

title: Cross-validation ranking stability — cv_ranking_stability
text: list components: settings List v, R, seed,       metric, family, backend, en_alpha,       lambda. rmse_mat \(R \times M\) matrix per-repeat mean       error values (RMSE log-loss depending metric). rank_mat \(R \times M\) integer matrix per-repeat       model ranks (rank 1 = best). summary Data frame columns model,       mean_rmse, sd_rmse, mean_rank,       top1_frequency, ordered mean rank.  Note: columns       mean_rmse sd_rmse store mean standard       deviation chosen error metric (RMSE log-loss); column       names retained backwards compatibility.

title: Perturb a dataset — perturb_data
text: Generates perturbed version dataset using one three strategies: bootstrap resampling, subsampling without replacement, Gaussian noise injection.

title: Perturb a dataset — perturb_data
code: perturb_data(   data,   method = c("bootstrap", "subsample", "noise"),   frac = 0.8,   noise_sd = 0.05,   response_col = NULL )

title: Perturb a dataset — perturb_data
text: data data frame. method Character string specifying perturbation method. One "bootstrap" (default), "subsample", "noise". frac Fraction rows retain subsampling. Ignored methods. Must \((0, 1]\). Default 0.8. noise_sd Noise level fraction column's standard deviation. Ignored unless method = "noise". Default 0.05. response_col Optional character string naming response (outcome) column exclude noise injection.  Useful want perturb predictors leave outcome unchanged.  Ignored method = "bootstrap" method = "subsample".  NULL (default) numeric columns including response receive noise.

title: Perturb a dataset — perturb_data
text: data frame columns data. number   rows equals nrow(data) bootstrap noise,   floor(frac * nrow(data)) subsampling.

title: Perturb a dataset — perturb_data
code: set.seed(1) d_boot <- perturb_data(mtcars, method = "bootstrap") d_sub  <- perturb_data(mtcars, method = "subsample", frac = 0.7) d_nois <- perturb_data(mtcars, method = "noise", noise_sd = 0.1)  # Perturb predictors only, leave the response (mpg) unchanged: d_pred_only <- perturb_data(mtcars, method = "noise",                             noise_sd = 0.1, response_col = "mpg")

text: diag_obj reprostat object run_diagnostics.

title: Reproducibility index — reproducibility_index
text: Computes composite reproducibility index (RI) 0–100 scale aggregating normalized versions coefficient, p-value, selection, prediction stability.

title: Reproducibility index — reproducibility_index
code: reproducibility_index(diag_obj)

title: Reproducibility index — reproducibility_index
text: diag_obj reprostat object run_diagnostics.

title: Reproducibility index — reproducibility_index
text: list components: index Scalar RI 0–100 scale. components Named numeric vector four sub-scores:       coef, pvalue, selection, prediction.       pvalue NA backend = "glmnet";       selection always available.

title: Reproducibility index — reproducibility_index
text: component mapped \([0, 1]\) follows: Coefficient component (\(C_\beta\)) \(C_\beta = \frac{1}{p}\sum_{j=1}^{p}     \exp\!\left(-S_{\beta,j} / (|\hat\beta_j^{(0)}| +     \bar\beta)\right)\),     \(\bar\beta = \max(\mathrm{median}_j|\hat\beta_j^{(0)}|,\, 10^{-4})\)     global scale reference.  prevents exponential     collapsing zero weakly-identified (near-zero) predictors.     Includes model terms (intercept predictors). P-value component (\(C_p\)) \(C_p = \frac{1}{p'}\sum_{j \neq \text{intercept}} |2 S_{p,j} - 1|\),     \(S_{p,j}\) proportion perturbation iterations     term \(j\) significant level alpha \(p'\)     number predictor terms.  Values near 0 1 (consistent     decisions) score high; 0.5 (random) scores zero.     NA backend = "glmnet". Selection component (\(C_\mathrm{sel}\)) "lm", "glm", "rlm": mean     sign consistency across predictors — proportion     perturbation iterations predictor's estimated sign     agrees base-fit sign.  Captures stability effect direction,     distinct significance stability (\(C_p\)).     "glmnet": mean non-zero selection frequency —     proportion perturbation iterations predictor's     coefficient non-zero.  Always available (never NA).     Excludes intercept. Prediction component (\(C_\mathrm{pred}\)) \(C_\mathrm{pred} = \exp(-\bar S_\mathrm{pred} /     (\mathrm{Var}(y) + \varepsilon))\).     Prediction variance relative outcome variance drives decay. RI \(100 \times (C_\beta + C_p + C_\mathrm{sel} + C_\mathrm{pred}) / k\), \(k\) number non-NA components.  backend = "glmnet", \(C_p\) NA RI based three components (\(k = 3\)): \(C_\beta\), \(C_\mathrm{sel}\), \(C_\mathrm{pred}\).  backends contribute four components (\(k = 4\)). Comparability across backends: "glmnet" uses three components "lm"/"glm"/"rlm" use four, RI values directly comparable across backends.

title: Reproducibility index — reproducibility_index
code: set.seed(1) d <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, B = 50) reproducibility_index(d) #> $index #> [1] 97.50225 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.9562648  0.9600000  1.0000000  0.9838253  #>

what: Bootstrap confidence interval for the reproducibility index — ri_confidence_interval
title: Bootstrap confidence interval for the reproducibility index — ri_confidence_interval
text: Estimates uncertainty RI resampling perturbation iterations already stored reprostat object (additional model fitting).

title: Bootstrap confidence interval for the reproducibility index — ri_confidence_interval
code: ri_confidence_interval(diag_obj, level = 0.95, R = 1000L, seed = NULL)

title: Bootstrap confidence interval for the reproducibility index — ri_confidence_interval
text: diag_obj reprostat object run_diagnostics. level Confidence level, e.g. 0.95 95% interval. Default 0.95. R Number bootstrap resamples perturbation draws. Default 1000. Values 300–500 sufficient uses. seed Integer random seed passed set.seed, NULL (default) leave global RNG state undisturbed. Pass integer fully reproducible intervals.

title: Bootstrap confidence interval for the reproducibility index — ri_confidence_interval
text: named numeric vector length 2 giving lower upper   quantile bounds RI bootstrap distribution.

title: Bootstrap confidence interval for the reproducibility index — ri_confidence_interval
code: set.seed(1) d <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, B = 50) ri_confidence_interval(d, R = 200, seed = 1) #>     2.5%    97.5%  #> 95.75155 98.61878

title: Run reproducibility diagnostics — run_diagnostics
text: Fits model original data, repeatedly fits perturbed versions collect coefficient estimates, p-values, predictions downstream stability analysis.  Four modeling backends supported: ordinary least squares ("lm"), generalized linear models ("glm"), robust regression ("rlm" via MASS), penalized regression ("glmnet" via glmnet).

title: Run reproducibility diagnostics — run_diagnostics
code: run_diagnostics(   formula,   data,   B = 200,   method = c("bootstrap", "subsample", "noise"),   alpha = 0.05,   frac = 0.8,   noise_sd = 0.05,   predict_newdata = NULL,   family = NULL,   backend = c("lm", "glm", "rlm", "glmnet"),   en_alpha = 1,   lambda = NULL,   perturb_response = FALSE )

title: Run reproducibility diagnostics — run_diagnostics
text: formula model formula. data data frame. B Integer. Number perturbation iterations. Default 200. method Perturbation method passed perturb_data. One "bootstrap" (default), "subsample", "noise". alpha Significance threshold p-value selection stability. Default 0.05. frac Subsampling fraction. Passed perturb_data. code: set.seed(1) # Linear model (small B for a quick check) diag_lm <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, B = 20) print(diag_lm) #> ReproStat Diagnostics #> --------------------- #> Formula   : mpg ~ wt + hp  #> Backend   : lm  #> Method    : bootstrap  #> Iterations: 20  #> Terms     : (Intercept), wt, hp   # \donttest{ # Logistic regression diag_glm <- run_diagnostics(am ~ wt + hp + qsec, data = mtcars, B = 50,                             family = stats::binomial()) reproducibility_index(diag_glm) #> $index #> [1] 75.00492 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.2497427  1.0000000  0.8533333  0.8971209  #>   # Robust regression (requires MASS) if (requireNamespace("MASS", quietly = TRUE)) {   diag_rlm <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, B = 50,                               backend = "rlm")   reproducibility_index(diag_rlm) } #> $index #> [1] 96.84662 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.9190617  0.9800000  1.0000000  0.9748032  #>   # Penalized regression / LASSO (requires glmnet) if (requireNamespace("glmnet", quietly = TRUE)) {   diag_glmnet <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp + qsec, data = mtcars,                                  B = 50, backend = "glmnet", en_alpha = 1)   reproducibility_index(diag_glmnet) } #> $index #> [1] 82.57931 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.7604751         NA  0.7450000  0.9719042  #>  # }

title: ReproStat 0.1.0
text: run_diagnostics(): main entry point supporting "lm", "glm", "rlm" (via MASS), "glmnet" (via glmnet) backends; three perturbation methods ("bootstrap", "subsample", "noise"). New argument perturb_response (default FALSE) controls whether response column perturbed noise method. perturb_data(): standalone data perturbation bootstrap, subsampling, Gaussian noise injection. New argument response_col allows response column excluded noise perturbation. coef_stability(): variance coefficient estimates across perturbation iterations. pvalue_stability(): proportion iterations predictor significant; intercept excluded output. selection_stability(): sign consistency estimated coefficients "lm" / "glm" / "rlm" backends; non-zero selection frequency "glmnet" backend. Intercept excluded. genuinely distinct measure pvalue_stability(). prediction_stability(): pointwise prediction variance across perturbation iterations. Coefficient component (c_beta) now uses global scale reference (median(|base_coef|)) instead hard-coded epsilon, preventing score collapsing near-zero coefficients. c_p (p-value stability) c_sel (selection stability) now genuinely distinct components; previously computed quantity. backend = "glmnet", selection component (c_sel) now mean non-zero selection frequency always available (previously NA). RI glmnet therefore based three components instead two. ri_confidence_interval(): bootstrap confidence interval RI. seed argument now defaults NULL, leaving caller’s global RNG state undisturbed. Pass integer fix seed explicitly. cv_ranking_stability(): repeated K-fold CV ranking stability model comparison across four backends. plot_stability(), plot_cv_stability(): base-graphics visualisations. plot_stability_gg(), plot_cv_stability_gg(): optional ggplot2-based equivalents (require ggplot2).
